attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Turn on the lamp with the remote control
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The provided images depict a scene containing a lamp and a sofa with several objects on it. However, none of the objects visible in the images match the types defined in the given PDDL domain file (`microwave`, `fridge`, `sink`, `knife`, or `receptacle` as defined for holding objects like those types). The domain is specifically designed for kitchen appliance interactions (heating, cooling, cleaning, slicing) and does not include predicates or actions for interacting with lamps or remote controls.

Specifically:

1.  **Objects and Types:**
    *   **Lamp:** A table lamp with a green, ribbed, bell-shaped lampshade and a brown base is visible. It is not defined as an object type in the domain.
    *   **Sofa:** A light green, three-seater sofa is present. It is not defined as a `receptacle` in the domain, although it is holding objects.
    *   **Red Pillow:** A red, square pillow is on the left cushion of the sofa. It is not defined in the domain.
    *   **Remote Control:** A white remote control with colored buttons is on the right cushion of the sofa. It is not defined as an `object` or `receptacle` in the domain.
    *   **Book/Magazine:** A small, rectangular object, possibly a book or magazine, is on the middle cushion of the sofa. It is not defined in the domain.
    *   **Blue Card:** A small, blue rectangular card is next to the book/magazine. It is not defined in the domain.

2.  **Quantities:**
    *   1 Lamp
    *   1 Sofa
    *   1 Red Pillow
    *   1 Remote Control
    *   1 Book/Magazine
    *   1 Blue Card

3.  **Spatial Relationships:**
    *   The lamp is positioned on the floor, separate from the sofa.
    *   The red pillow, book/magazine, blue card, and remote control are all located on the cushions of the sofa.
    *   The remote control is on the rightmost cushion.
    *   The book/magazine and blue card are on the middle cushion.
    *   The red pillow is on the leftmost cushion.

4.  **Current State:**
    *   The lamp is currently off (not illuminated).
    *   The remote control is resting on the sofa, not being held by any agent.
    *   No objects are being held by an agent.
    *   No receptacles (as defined in the domain) are open or closed.
    *   No objects are clean, hot, cool, sliced, or toggled according to the domain predicates.

5.  **Visual Properties:**
    *   The lampshade is olive green with a ribbed texture and a black trim.
    *   The lamp base is brown.
    *   The sofa is a light, yellowish-green color with a textured fabric.
    *   The pillow is a solid, bright red.
    *   The remote control is white with a grid of colored buttons (red, green, yellow, blue).
    *   The floor is a light wood laminate.

In summary, the scene contains objects that are not represented in the provided PDDL domain. The instruction to "Turn on the lamp with the remote control" cannot be executed using the defined actions and predicates, as there is no `ToggleObject` action defined for a lamp, nor is a remote control defined as an `object` that can be used to toggle another object. The domain lacks the necessary components to model this task.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
The provided PDDL domain file is designed for a specific set of kitchen-related tasks (heating, cooling, cleaning, slicing) and does not contain the necessary predicates, actions, or object types to model the interaction with a lamp or a remote control as described in the instruction.

Specifically:
- The domain defines `microwave`, `fridge`, and `sink` as `receptacle` types, but does not define `lamp` or `remote` as any object type.
- The `ToggleObject` action is defined, but its precondition requires the agent to be `atLocation` of the object to be toggled. There is no `lamp` object defined to be toggled.
- There is no action defined for using a `remote control` to toggle another object.
- The `isOn` predicate is defined, but there is no mechanism in the domain to link the `remote control` to the `lamp` for toggling.

Therefore, it is impossible to generate a valid PDDL problem file for the instruction "Turn on the lamp with the remote control" using the given domain file, as the domain lacks the required components to represent the objects and the action needed to achieve the goal.

The PDDL problem cannot be generated.

    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Turn on the lamp with the remote control
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene contains a living room environment with several objects, but only a subset are relevant to the provided domain and the instruction "Turn on the lamp with the remote control".

1.  **Objects and their types:**
    -   **Remote Control:** One object, visually identifiable as a standard remote control with multiple buttons. It is an `object` as per the domain (not a receptacle).
    -   **Lamp:** One object, consisting of a brown base and a green, textured, bell-shaped lampshade. It is an `object` as per the domain.
    -   **Couch:** One object, a light green sofa with three seat cushions and a red pillow on the left. The couch is a `receptacle` as it can hold items (the remote and other objects are resting on it).

2.  **Quantities:**
    -   `object`: 2 (Remote Control, Lamp)
    -   `receptacle`: 1 (Couch)

3.  **Spatial Relationships:**
    -   The `Remote Control` is `inReceptacle` the `Couch`. It is resting on the rightmost seat cushion.
    -   The `Lamp` is positioned on the floor, to the right of the couch (partially visible in the first image).
    -   The robot agent is not currently at any object's location.

4.  **Current State:**
    -   The `Remote Control` is not being held by the agent and is resting on the couch.
    -   The `Lamp` is not currently `isOn` (as the instruction is to turn it on).
    -   The `Couch` is not `opened` (it is not an openable receptacle).

5.  **Visual Properties:**
    -   The **Remote Control** is silver/grey with a blue button visible on its front.
    -   The **Lamp** has a brown, blocky base and a green lampshade with a ribbed texture and a black trim.
    -   The **Couch** is a light, lime-green color with a textured fabric surface. A red, square pillow with a button tuft is on the left seat cushion.
    -   The floor is a light wood laminate.
    -   The background wall is a plain, light grey color.

The task requires the agent to interact with the remote control to toggle the lamp's state. The remote control is currently on the couch, and the lamp is on the floor nearby. The agent must first go to the couch to pick up the remote, then go to the lamp to toggle it.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem turn_on_lamp_with_remote)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        remote_control - object
        lamp - object
        couch - receptacle
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation agent1 couch) ; Agent starts at the couch where the remote is.
        (inReceptacle remote_control couch)
        (not (isOn lamp))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (holds agent1 remote_control))
    )
    (:goal (and (isOn lamp) (isToggled lamp)))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem turn_on_lamp_with_remote)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        remote_control - object
        lamp - object
        couch - receptacle
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation agent1 couch) ; Agent starts at the couch where the remote is.
        (inReceptacle remote_control couch)
        (not (isOn lamp))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (holds agent1 remote_control))
    )
    (:goal (and (isOn lamp) (isToggled lamp)))
)

final.problem:
(define (problem turn_on_lamp_with_remote)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        remote_control - object
        lamp - object
        couch - receptacle
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation agent1 couch) ; Agent starts at the couch where the remote is.
        (inReceptacle remote_control couch)
        (not (isOn lamp))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (holds agent1 remote_control))
    )
    (:goal (and (isOn lamp) (isToggled lamp)))
)
